event.name: FR_WARZONE_MIXED_SIGNAL
event.text: It appears that the enemy ship is sending out a distress signal to a nearby fleet! Though, since both fleets are fighting each other, letting this signal go through would mean the enemy would also be targeted.
When no ASB activates, you assume that it's a good sign that your augment has functioned properly, and you continue to fight.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_HOSTILE_SIGNAL
event.text: It appears the enemy ship is sending out a distress signal to a nearby fleet! Brace for impact!
When no ASB activates, you assume that it's a good sign that your augment has functioned properly, and you continue to fight.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_ALLY_SIGNAL
event.text: It appears that an ally ship is sending out a distress signal to a nearby fleet! You could intercept it if you don't need the backup.
When no ASB activates, you assume that it's a good sign that your augment has functioned properly, and you continue to fight.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_FEDERATION_HQ

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: The location of the Federation Research HQ.
beaconType.visitedTooltip: Explored location. Nothing left of interest.

event.text: You arrive at the Federation Research Headquarters, or at least what's left of it, though it seems to be in surprisingly good condition given it was atacked by a whole fleet not too long ago. A small Federation scout scans your ship just to make sure you are not a threat before flying away towards the base.

The scout leads you to the landing pad of the facility; the journey is quick and uneventful, with the radio eeriely quiet. After landing your ship and disembarking, you are greeted by worn Defender Engi with a missing arm and a cracked visor, flanked by 2 Federation elite soldiers.
"Federation Renegade, welcome. I am: Lead Scientist HS-98A. Specialization = Flagship model ships. I was in charge of: Project Retribution." The Engi explains, despite the fact that their broken voice synthesizer is struggling to keep up with their thoughts.
"Project was = Near completion when Rebel forces attacked. They stole: Prototype Flagship made for the Federation. We were forced to: Teach them how to pilot it or else we would have been all killed." The Engi's tone seems emotionless as usual, but you could imagine a tinge of regret in their synthetic voice.

choice.text: Let me guess, you want us to retrieve the ship?

event.text:
The Engi nods robotically. "That is what this unit hopes, yes. If the flagship were to be adapted by the Rebellion, then it's this unit's fear that it may doom our reality." The Engi speaks in a tone that almost sounds grim.
"I know Renegades do not work for price = free. Thus I purpose a: mutually beneficial arrengement. You clear the flagship of rebels while keeping the ship = intact and you may keep the ship's: blueprints. How does this offer sound?"

The Engi nods. "Very well. Luckily we can track: the Flagship. We will add a marker to: your map." HS-98A transmits the coordinates to your ship before shutting off for a moment, rebooting a few seconds later.

The Engi nods sluggishly. "My form = Irrevelent in the face of the Rebellion." Suddenly, the Engi stops for a moment before they turn back to you hastily. "There seems to be another issue, Renegade. The Coalition has also sent a flagship of their own. It seems to be: Attacking Rebels for now. However, once the Rebels are dealt with, they will surely turn their guns towards: The Federation. Would you deal with this threat as well? This unit is sure that you might also receive: compensation for it."
The Engi nods before their body slowly shuts down, though this time they don't turn themselves back on. The guards thank you for your help and let you be on your way.

choice.text: We have enough on our plate. We must pass.

event.text: The Engi nods slowly shutting off, this time for good. The soldiers guide you back to your ship and you set off with your target in mind.

choice.text: That sounds too dangerous, we can't risk it.

event.text: The Engi nods before allowing you and your crew to leave.

choice.text: This is suspicious, avoid the ship and prepare to jump away.

event.text: Knowing how dangerous this sector is, trusting any random ship, even Federation ones, is a risk too great to take right now.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_FEDERATION_HQ_FEDERATION_FLAGSHIP

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: Location of the stolen Federation Flagship.
beaconType.visitedTooltip: Explored location. Nothing left of interest.

event.text: You've tracked down the location of the stolen Federation flagship; it seems to be in the middle of a rebel hanger. Security is surprisingly light as well, though you doubt it will stay like that for long.

You charge fowards, forcing the ship to engage you. It seems the rebels didn't have enough time to fully paint over the ship in Rebel colors. Still, it seems its weapons are fully online. You are suddenly hailed by the flagship. On your screen appears a rebel wearing an aviator helmet covering their bright red hair. "So you are the Renegade the Federation sent to stop us, huh? That's very cute. Our cause is too great to be stopped by a brown-nosed Federation dog like you. Be ready to feel the wrath of the Rebellion! For I, Victor, will strike you down!"

choice.text: (Vance Prime) "Halt, soldier! I order you to evacuate the flagship and deliver it back to the Federation's HQ in one piece!"

event.text: Victor blinks before their face contorts in pure anger. "You traitorous rat! You left us to tail back to the crumbling hive of filth that is the Federation! You are no Rebel! You are a disgrace, a disease! One I will gladly eliminate!" Victor turns off the feed and attacks, leaving Vance fuming.
Attacking this ship is akin to throwing a stone at a hornet's nest. You decide you like living and so you quickly leave before being detected.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_FEDERATION_HQ_FEDERATION_FLAGSHIP_DEADCREW
event.text: As the last remaining rebels fall, you know you don't have any seconds to waste. The rebels have certainly asked for reinforcements before they died, meaning that you need to be quick if you want to get the blueprints.

It takes a minute to find the right schematics, but you manage to get them. It's a shame the project won't be deployed soon, but at least you prevented the rebels from ever using it. You also locate a unique modified gun on your way out. It seems reserved for only the highest-ranking Federation soldiers, but you figure it should be fine to take it.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_FEDERATION_HQ_COALITION_FLAGSHIP

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: Location of the stolen Coalition Flagship.
beaconType.visitedTooltip: Explored location. Nothing left of interest.

event.text: You've tracked down the location of the Coalition flagship, seemingly focused on attacking a small Rebel outpost. There's barely any ships defending it aside from a few technician ships, they won't last long without your help.

You charge onwards, forcing the flagship to engage you. You receive a transmission from the flagship; on the other end is no less than a rock elder!
"Ah, the weak and pathetic Federation ship; I've heard a lot about your struggle. You haven't got an idea of what you are signing up for, have you? I'm Rockmore, the Elder in charge of the Inquisition's research, and I'm here to make sure both the Rebels and the Federation are without any technology that might aid them against the Crusade. I will enjoy watching you struggle, little fly."

choice.text: The rebels can fend for themselves.

event.text: They were the ones that started this mess; this is karma in your book.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_FEDERATION_HQ_COALITION_FLAGSHIP_DESTROYED
event.text: The massive flagship cracks in half before exploding. While you did save the technicians, the blueprints of the flagship are now lost forever.

You are soon hailed by the technician dressed in MV Rebel colors from the outpost. "Renegade, thank the heavens you arrived! The flagship, it attacked us suddenly! This outpost wasn't meant for combat; it was simply a place to repair our auto-ships. The few ships we had defending us were destroyed in seconds... I can't imagine what would have happened if you hadn't got here."
The rebel clears his throat. "My name is Jon Briggs. I'm the lead technician here, sent by Universe 1 to help train the new generation of technicians. Though, seeing all the carnage the Rebellion did here with my own eyes... I fear I'm not cut out for this job anymore. Would you mind if I joined your crew?"

event.name: FR_WARZONE_FEDERATION_HQ_COALITION_FLAGSHIP_DEADCREW
event.text: Rockmore puts up a good fight, being the last one to fall; however, even his mighty hammer can't stop you. You quickly locate the control panel and download the blueprints before scrapping the ship. You also find a unique weapon, one much more powerful than the standard variant, problably reserved for only the mightiest of Coalition soldiers.

You are soon hailed by the technician dressed in MV Rebel colors from the outpost. "Renegade, thank the heavens you arrived! The flagship, it attacked us suddenly! This outpost wasn't meant for combat; it was simply a place to repair our auto-ships. The few ships we had defending us were destroyed in seconds... I can't imagine what would have happened if you hadn't got here."
The rebel clears his throat. "My name is Jon. I'm the lead technician here, sent by Universe 1 to help train the new generation of technicians. Though, seeing all the carnage the Rebellion did here with my own eyes... I fear I'm not cut out for this job anymore. Would you mind if I joined your crew?"

destroyed.text:
The mighty pirate ship cracks in half, its advanced design clearly reaching its limits. Still, some mechanism in its construction prevents its total destruction, with only half the ship blowing up.
You first scrap the destroyed half; in it, you find the highly advanced weapon Purple Beard used against you, still intact even after such an explosion! The other laser seems to have been badly damaged during the fight, however, to the point where it can no longer be used.

deadCrew.text:
Purple Beard was an exceptional opponent, but even he had his limits. The fight is long and painful but eventually the last of his crew falls, leaving the rest of his ship ready for scrapping.
The first thing you attempt to recover is his powerful lasers; however, it seems one of them was damaged badly during the fight and can't be recovered. At least the other one is in perfect condition.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_PURPLE_BEARD
event.text:
As soon as you jump into this beacon you are immediately hailed by the only other ship at this beacon... at least, apart from the giant fleet in the background. "Renegade... So it seems our paths have finally crossed..." The man on the screen is the legendary corsair Purple Beard, the only unaffiliated pirate to ever reach such a rank.
His voice is slow and heavy; even though he avoids looking at you in the eyes, you can still feel a tremandous weight behind his own. His apperance reinforces the legends told about him in the less savory places of the multiverse, about an ex-Legionarre Rebel who betrayed his own after becoming disillusioned with the cause. With every faction wanting him dead for his past, the only path he could tread was that of a pirate.

choice.text: So you are the legendary Purple Beard?

event.text: The pirate winces at the name but slowly nods. "...Yes, I am... Though... I wish I weren't..." The man glances away. His eyes are visible for just a moment: one, a cybernetic replacement eye, and the other, a glass eye with a scar crossing it vertically. "And I suppose... you wish to kill me?"

The legend seems taken aback by your admission. "...you... do not?... Why? I am the number one most wanted criminal in all the Multiverse!" Once more, you catch a glimpse of his eyes. A mix of shock and perhaps a bit of hope seems to show.

choice.text: We are Renegades; our reputation isn't much better than yours in some parts.

event.text: The man lets out a dry chuckle before coughing violently. Once he recovers, he once again speaks. "... I see... So... if you do not wish to kill me... what do you want?"

choice.text: You don't seem to enjoy being a pirate. Why not join our crew and become a hero?

event.text: Purple Beard freezes for a moment, his eyes now fully visible. A slight tear forms on their corners before it is swiftly removed with his aged sleeves. He smiles slightly as he finally looks at you directly in the eyes. "...Heh... If you aren't the weirdest Renegade I've ever met... Truth be told... I always wished I could... start over... Very well, Renegade... I accept your offer... However... I can't just leave... A legend like me deserves a good death... Tell you what... you will fight me and my crew... Feel free to be as violent as you wish... make it something worthy of my legend... Once the fight ends, scrap this ship... I have a plan... En garde..."
The pirate sighs. "...Very well... Good luck... Renegade..." The feed cuts and the pirate's weapons go online.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_PURPLE_BEARD_WIN
event.text: The pirate fleet suddunly stops dead in their tracks as they realize the legend has been defeated. You are soon hailed by a giant rockman outcast inside the pirate fleet. "You... you killed him... I can barely belive my eyes! Renegade! I don't know if you realize what you have just done, but by killing Purple Beard, you will now take his place in our legends!" She then hails all other fleet ships. "Everyone! Stop attacking the Renegade at once! They are now our leader!" You are suddunly flooded with hails from every pirate ship in the beacon, all singing songs in your honor. It isn't too long before a small pirate shuttle delivers a box of supplies to you. It seems piracy pays off... sometimes.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_PURPLE_BEARD_WIN_PLAN
event.text: The pirate fleet suddunly stops dead in their tracks as they realize the legend has been defeated. You are soon hailed by a giant rockman outcast inside the pirate fleet. "You... you killed him... I can barely belive my eyes! Renegade! I don't know if you realize what you have just done, but by killing Purple Beard, you will now take his place in our legends!" She then hails all other fleet ships. "Everyone! Stop attacking the Renegade at once! They are now our leader!" You are suddunly flooded with hails from every pirate ship in the beacon, all singing songs in your honor. It isn't too long before a small pirate shuttle delivers a box of supplies to you. It seems piracy pays off... sometimes.

You are almost done scrapping the rest of the ship when you see a sealed room hidden so well you almost missed it. After opening it, you are greated by small hidden clonebay which just finished cloning Purple Beard! After bringing him back to your ship, and letting him heal a bit, he approaches you with a wide grin. "Renegade! It's... so nice to see you alive and well after our fight..." He chuckles painfully and taps your shoulder with more strength than you expect from someone his age.

He nods slowly. "Now that everyone thinks I kicked the bucket... my legend can finally be put to rest... and... as thanks for giving me a new life... I will help you along your journey... Renegade... or should I say... 'Pirate Legend'..." He winks and laughs dryly.

destroyed.text: As you watch the Coalition vessel break apart, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: As their last crew falls, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_STORE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_COALITION
event.text: A nearby Federation store is under fire by a R.U.E.S. vessel. Their defenses are quickly crumbling; they won't last much longer without your help!
You prepare for combat.
Fortunately, the ship remains oblivious to your presence.

destroyed.text: You've destroyed the Rebel Elite's ship: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've wiped out the crew of the Rebel Elite: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet though, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_STORE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_REBEL
event.text: A nearby Federation store is under fire by an MFK Ace ship. They are trying to fight back but they are getting overwhelmed quickly.
You prepare for combat.
Fortunately, the ship remains oblivious to your presence.
It seems that the cargo of the Elite Federation station survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_STORE_WARZONE_FEDERATION
event.text: A nearby Elite Federation outpost is surprisingly still open for business, despite the fact that it's in the middle of this warzone. The fleet is problably giving them enough protection to not be on full lockdown.
Before you shop, you still have some time to do something else aboard the ship.

As you attempt to dock onto the station, your sensors notice its weapons have suddenly gone online! "We've heard about all your misdeeds, Renegade! We won't let you make our job harder than it already is!"
"A Federation soldier! You must be a very important vessel! Since we have a surplus of weapons, we will gladly give you one as a token of graditude."
"Admiral Tully? It's an honor to finally meet you! Please, have this weapon as a gift and feel free to peruse our store!"
Nothing will stop you from being a merciless ravager of all you come across. Hopefully you didn't pick a fight you can't win.

surrender.text: "Alright! We get it, you are very tough! We will let you trade just do it quickly!"

event.text: The R.U.E.S. station powers its weapons off. You're worried this is some kind of trap, but you are too far to back off now.
Before you shop, you still have some time to do something else aboard the ship.

choice.text: Refuse and finish the station off.

event.name: FR_STORE_WARZONE_COALITION
event.text: Looks like the Coalition built an elite forward outpost in this beacon, probably to support their offensive into this sector. Security is tight, with R.U.E.S. fleets all around the station. Maybe you could pass the ship off as a Coalition spy if you have the right crew?
Your crew has to work overtime to convince the guards, but eventually they allow you in.

"Coalition outpost, I demand access to your store as well as a parting gift in the form of a weapon. This is an order."
Whoever is in charge of the outpost scrambles to attention at the sight of the Queen. "O-Of course, your highness! Right this way!" The station leader allows you to shop in their outpost and then gifts you the weapon as ordered.

"Hey you sssslime bagssss! Let ussss sssshop in your sssstupid outposssst! And give ussss a new weapon too!"
The leader of the outpost freezes for a moment, "Very well, Captain. You may dock your ship." The station leader allows you to shop in their outpost and then gifts you the weapon as ordered.

"Excuse me, but I'm Tyrdeo, the President of the Republic. As one of the leaders of the Coalition, I ask that you allow me and my crew to shop in this establishment. Also, as I'm sure you have heard, leaders of the Coalition are entitled to one free weapon in any Coalition outpost."
The leader of the outpost tilts their head for a moment before shrugging. "Very well, President. You may dock your ship." The station leader allows you to shop in their outpost and then gifts you the weapon as ordered.

choice.text: Fight the outpost; maybe they will respect your strength and let you trade?

event.text: You prepare to fight the outpost. As expected, they send out a request for ASB backup from the nearby fleet.
When no ASB activates, you assume that it's a good sign that your augment has functioned properly, and you continue to fight.
It's a total waste, but you don't have anything to sneak in, and attacking the outpost would be too risky with the fleet surrounding it.

destroyed.text: The outpost has been destroyed. While there are plenty of supplies left behind from the wreck itself, any extra supplies in its cargo have surely been destroyed.

deadCrew.text: The crew of the outpost have been eliminated. You scrap the ship's exterior before investigating inside.

event.text: Fortunately, it appears the Rebels had a good supply of resources saved up, all of which now belong to you.

event.name: FR_STORE_WARZONE_REBEL
event.text: It seems the Rebels are eager to make themselves at home in this sector. A trading outpost has already been established at the beacon, probably to serve as a checkpoint for passing Rebel ships as well as the fleet that surrounds it. Realistically, only an elite rebel crew would get anywhere with them.

choice.text: (Technician) Pretend to be a Rebel vessel.

event.text: Your Technician manages to fake it well enough for the outpost to believe your lie. They let you trade as well as give you some supplies.

choice.text: (MFK Ace Crew) Pretend to be a Rebel vessel.

event.text: Your MFK Ace manages to fake it well enough for the outpost to believe your lie. They let you trade as well as give you some supplies.

choice.text: (Vance Prime) "Hail, loyal soldier! Allow us to dock and shop, this is an order!"

event.text: "O-Oh shit! Of course sir! Right this way!" The soldiers in the station all scramble to get the outpost ready for their leader. One of the MFK Aces even offers to join your ship.

choice.text: (Vance One) "Hail, soldier! Allow us to dock and shop, this is an order!"

event.text: The MFK Ace soldier in charge of communications blnks before shouting, "ONE?! What the hell are you doing here?! Whatever your plan is, we won't allow it to happen! Fellas, to your battle stations! We have a rat to squish!" The outpost goes hostile and refuse any more hails.

choice.text: Attack the station; maybe they have backup supplies!

event.text: You power up the weapons and engage the outpost.
You fly past the outpost, deciding not to start more conflict in this already hostile sector.

destroyed.text: No matter how influential they were, at the end of the day they were still pirates. As you watch their ill-gotten station crumble to dust, you breath a sigh of relief that they won't make this sector even more of a hellish place.

deadCrew.text: Not even the toughest pirates can stop you now. That's one less plague to torment this sector.

event.name: FR_STORE_WARZONE_PIRATE
event.text:
It seems even this battle-ravaged place isn't safe from piracy. A group of very influential and wealthy pirates decided to set up a black market store in this sector, potentially hoping to score some easy sales or salvage from damaged vessels. These pirates don't seem like the usual lot either, so be careful when dealing with them.
As you approach the station you see what appears to be a pirate fleet. How those unaffiliated pirates even managed to steal multiple fleet ships is beyond you, but you doubt they are just for show.
Some elite smugglers welcome you inside and offer some less-than-legal services and goods.

The pirates immediately notice your Angel crew, with the leader of the store hailing you, "Well well well, what do we have here? One of Vael's top brass? Now, we wouldn't want to pick a fight with him, so about we give you a little something something and you keep anything that you see here to yourself?"

The pirates immediately notice the pirate leader as soon as he enters the view of the feed, with the leader of the store hailing you, "Well turn me sideways and call me porky! If it isn't the legendary Purple Beard himself! Didn't know we were in the presence of legends! Let me be your own personal tour guide of our little 'fixer-upper', and please, have this little gift, as a little gift between fellow pirates."
These scum are still outlaws, regardless if they're running a market or not. Time to bring their villainy to an end.
You aren't looking to make any black market deals.

event.name: FR_ITEM_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GIFT
event.text: An Elite Federation ship hails you. "Renegade! This sector is crawling with people who want us dead. In times like these, we must look out for each other. We have an extra weapon that we picked up from an MFK Ace ship a few jumps back; we have no use for it, so you might as well have it."
The Federation captain smiles and sends over the weapon alongside a small package of resources.

The Federation captain is about to send over the weapon when they realize who you are. "Wait a moment! You are that blasted renegade that has been causing us problems ever since you arrived here! Scum like you deserve only death!"
The captain is shocked by your sudden betrayal. "Turncoats! I should have known your kind isn't to be trusted!"
You thank the captain and assure them that you are well-equipped enough for the sector. The captain is confused but accepts your choice either way.

event.name: FR_ITEM_WARZONE_DEBRIS
event.text: This beacon appears to have been the place of a massive skirmish between all three factions, with wrecks scattered all around it. A few of these wrecks haven't been picked apart by pirates yet, though it's only a matter of time before they come back for the rest. You have enough time to salvage one ship before said pirates arrive.

You quickly salvage the Unity ship. It's a shame that something with so much potential had to meet such a grim fate. To your surprise, inside the ship you find a survivor! They offer to join your crew after you rescue them.

You quickly salvage the MFK Ace ship. It seems like Vance's big scary fleet isn't as powerful as you thought. Inside the ship you find a strange laser weapon. You don't know where it came from or how the Rebels got it, but it seems to be really powerful.

You quickly salvage the R.U.E.S. ship. The Coalition is very protective of their elite ships, only deploying them against very high value targets. Inside the ship you find a modular version of one of their signature augments which can be installed and uninstalled on the go.
The chances of something going wrong are too high to risk salvage random wrecks.

event.name: FR_ITEM_WARZONE_PIRATE_TRADE
event.text: A elite pirate seems to have set up shop on this beacon. They hail you and offer you a random power-efficient weapon for a heavy discount. The catch is that you won't know what it is until you buy it. In the background, you also see what appears to be a pirate fleet, problably taking this opportunity to get easy tribute.
The pirate transfers a crate containing the weapon as soon as they validate your purchase. You worry you might have been dupped but thankfully this pirate was an honorable one all along.
The pirate isn't surprised by your action, as it seems they already powered their weapons before you even finished powering yours.
The pirate is slightly offended by your rejection of their deal, but they aren't reckless enough to attack you for it.

event.name: FR_ITEM_WARZONE_FEDERATION_AUTO_REPAIR
event.text: A Federation Auto ship seems to have been deployed in this sector as a way to help on the war effort, offering a combo of resources and repair for free to Federation allies.
The auto quickly transfers over a crate with the supplies before effortlessly repairing your ship completely.
The autoship scans your ship before quickly powering on its weapons. Seems like your notoriety preceeds you.
The auto ship quickly reacts to your aggression and powers its weapons.
The auto simply ends its transmission and moves on to find another ship to help.

event.name: FR_ITEM_WARZONE_LANIUS
event.text: This beacon seems really uneventful until you detect a a hail from a nearby Lanius shuttle. "Seek metal. Ship too small to hold metal. Join crew?".

choice.text: Allow the Lanius to join your crew.

event.text: The Lanius is ejected from the shuttle and quickly arrives on your ship.
The Lanius is very disappointed but they quickly jump away in search of a better vessel.

event.name: FR_ITEM_WARZONE_GENERIC
event.text: From the beacon, you can spot several large masses. Among them are two gigantic fields of debris, filled with the wrecks of all sorts of factions. You could salvage ships in this beacon but there's no telling what you will find.

choice.text: Explore the pile of metallic debris.

event.text: You decide not to spend any time examining these fields.
You search the debris field, but it seems like there's nothing of value here. Unfortunately, the debris is both loose and densely packed, so you'll have to avoid damage as you charge your FTL drive!
You search the debris field. Amidst the largely worthless junk, you manage to track down a hidden supply of missiles in a large pile of debris.
You search the debris field. While much of it is worthless junk, you manage to scrounge up a small amount of useful resources.

You search the debris field. The material itself is mostly worthless debris, but amidst the junk is a single Federation shuttle. It desperately hails you. "Please renegade, let me join your crew! This shuttle is almost out of fuel and oxygen!".

choice.text: Accept the Federation personnel onto your crew.

event.text: The crew breaths a sigh of relief as they enter your ship. "Those damn Rebels attacked us when we were vulnerable, I don't think anyone else survived..."

You search the debris field. The material itself is mostly worthless junk, but floating adrift in the field is a badly damaged Coalition scout. It desperately hails you. "Federation Renegade! If you allow me to join your vessel then I will do as you order!"

choice.text: Accept the Coalition personnel onto your crew.

event.text: The crew seems relieved as you let them onto your ship. "Finally. A surprisingly powerful pirate ship cornered us after the battle. They seemed to deploy some strange augment before battle that made our guns refuse to fire."

destroyed.text: You've destroyed the Rebel Elite's ship: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've wiped out the crew of the Rebel Elite: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

destroyed.text: As you watch the Coalition vessel break apart, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: As their last crew falls, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_REBEL_COALITION_BATTLE
event.text: A R.U.E.S. and an MFK Ace ship are in a heated dogfight on this beacon while their respective fleets fight in the background. You could pick a side to fight alongside but that will cause the fleets to also start targeting you.
The Coalition ship is caught off guard by your advances. It decides that the rebel ship is a lower threat than you and so turns their weapons towards your ship.
The Rebel manages to disable the coalition's weapons before seeing you aproach. They waste no time before targeting you with their next shot.

choice.text: Ignore them and prepare to jump away.

event.text: Messing with either side of this conflict is bound to cause issues. It's better to let them destroy each other.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_REBEL_COALITION_BATTLE_COALITION
event.text: The R.U.E.S. ship hails you. "A Federation renegade helping ussss? Now have I sssseen everything! I ssssuppousssse you desssserve a reward." The captain chuckles before cutting the feed.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_REBEL_COALITION_BATTLE_REBEL
event.text: The MFK Ace hails you. "I can't believe I'm saying this to a Federation dog, but thank you for the help. Those Coalition freaks are a lot tougher than expected. While it pains me to say this, you deserve a reward." The captain sighs before cutting the feed.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_PIRATE_FEDERATION
event.text: It seems that an elite pirate is in the middle of a dogfight with an elite Federation ship. The pirate is somehow coming out on top while the Federation fleet in the background is struggling to hit them, making the whole situation even more baffling.
The pirate captain chuckles as they welcome another challenger with open arms.
That pirate is problably on another level compared to you if it can do loops around an elite federation ship like that. Safer to just ignore them.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_PIRATE_FEDERATION_WIN
event.text: The Federation ship hails you. "Thank the stars you arrived, Renegade. I have no idea how those pirates were so experienced, fighting them felt like fighting an MFK Ace! We heard reports of other nasty pirates like those around this sector too... Once this is over, I'm going to report the existance of this 'elite pirates' thing to high command, this threat needs to be taken more seriously. Anyways, you deserve a reward for your bravery today, we will also be sure to spread the word of your courage to others."

destroyed.text: As you watch the Coalition vessel break apart, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: As their last crew falls, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_COALITION_UNION
event.text: As you enter this beacon, the ship is flooded with distress calls from the nearby moon. It seems the Coalition fleet here is attacking a mostly defenceless Union research station, with a R.U.E.S. destroyer leading the charge. A few Militia ships are attempting to buy the crew enough time to evacuate, but without help they won't make it.

The Coalition ship is caught off guard by the sudden reinforcements. Still they don't miss a beat and turn their weapons towards you. "A Federation Renegade?! What are you doing here?! You know what, I don't care that command thinks this is a waste of time, I will destroy your ship and then this waste of space in the form of a research station! Then I- I mean *we* will finally have a place to rest after each fight!"
Attacking the leader of a whole fleet would be a mistake greater than you can afford to do at the moment. Best to just move on and pretend you never saw any of this.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_COALITION_UNION_WIN
event.text: Without the destroyer leading the charge, the rest of the fleet scatters, seemingly under orders to retreat. You are hailed by a Union scientist. "Thank the heavens you arrived! Our brave security did what they could, but we would have never stood a chance against a whole fleet! Please take this augment; I'm sure it will come in hand in your mission."

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_REBEL_DAMAGED
event.text: Despite being close enough to be in ASB range from the MFK fleet, this beacon is surprisingly peaceful. The only thing of note is a damaged MFK Ace ship. Though the damage doesn't seem to have been done to any combat-essential areas. They notice you but make no hostile moves.
The MFK Ace ship struggles to power itself online for battle, but is quickly able to get its systems online enough to fight you.
If they didn't attack you first that means they don't want to fight you. And so, by the same coin, you decide to not fight them either.

destroyed.text: You've defeated the Innovation Auto-ship. The escalation in threat posed by this foe could not be denied, but in the end, it was still nothing more than an infernal machine promptly silenced by your show of force.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the advanced Auto-ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_AUTO_FIGHT
event.text: To your surprise you find two auto ships fighting in this rather secluded beacon, a Federation and a Innovation Rebel auto ship. The rebel one, being an elite ship, has the clear upper hand and will certainly destroy the Federation one if not stopped.
The auto ship swiftly responds to your aggression in kind, aiming their weapons directly at you!
They are just auto ships, one more or one less won't matter in the grand scheme of things.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_AUTO_FIGHT_WIN
event.text: The Federation auto hails, "Renegade. Your contributions have been noted and deemed worthy of a: High tier reward."

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_COALITION_NEUTRAL
event.text: As the R.U.E.S. fleet looms in the distance, you expect that a fight with a Coalition ship would happen at any moment, yet all that happens is you being hailed by a R.U.E.S. ship. "Oh for goodnesssss ssssake, *another* Federation sssship?! We jusssst desssstroyed a whole fleet you dumbassssses! You know what? I can't be bothered, you can leave." The slug waves you away.

choice.text: We need to avange the fallen! Attack the Coalition ship!

event.text: The slug groans and mumbles some expletives under their breath before powering on their weapons.
While the death of the Federation crew was a great shame, with the massive fleet in the background it's a terrible idea to attack the smug slug.

gotaway.text: The auto managed to jump away. Like clockwork you can already feel the fleet getting closer to you.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_REBEL_AUTO_ESCAPING
event.text: As soon as you jump into this beacon you detect that an FTL drive is starting to charge. When looking around you find an Innovation Rebel auto ship preparing to jump, no doubt heading to warn the rebel fleet if it gets away.

choice.text: We can't let it escape! Attack them!

event.text: The auto ship powers on its weapons but doesn't stop charging its FTL drive.
Innovation autos are tough nuts to crack; better let it escape than deal with its advanced systems and weapons.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_ENGINEER_REQUEST
event.text: It's rare to see Technicians outswide their core sectors, but it seems the Rebellion is getting desperate for support if they are willing to call on their help. The Technician ship doesn't attack you though, instead they hail you. "Renegade, I know this is unorthodox, but we are heavily behind our quotas and we need to raise scrap quickly. We don't have any equipment to sell you but we are willing to let you hire one of our trained personnel."
The Field Technician thanks you and beams abord their crew. They ramble on about the terms of their employment but nothing jumps out to you as predatory so you sign it without much worry.
The Technician seems to start panicking; they weren't expecting you to decline their deal. "I'm sorry Renegade, but we are too far behind to be able to miss out on a deal like this. Weapons up."

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_GENERIC_REBEL
event.text: You find an MFK Ace ship patroling the nearby beacon, though they haven't noticed you yet.
The enemy ship quickly reacts to your arrival, powering up their weapons in kind.
You manage to stay under the radar of the enemy ship long enough for your FTL drive to charge.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_GENERIC_COALITION
event.text: You find a R.U.E.S. vessel adrift in this beacon. Their systems are intact and you detect crew inside. They are problably just taking a break.
The enemy ship quickly reacts to your arrival, powering up their weapons in kind.
You manage to stay under the radar of the enemy ship long enough for your FTL drive to charge.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_GENERIC_FEDERATION
event.text: You find a Unity ship protecting an important trading route. They scan your ship and allow you to pass undisturbed.
The enemy ship quickly reacts to your aggression, powering up their weapons in kind.
The ship eventually stops tracking you, letting you charge your FTL drive uninhibited.

event.name: FR_NEUTRAL_WARZONE_GENERIC_PIRATE
event.text: You find an elite pirate ship quickly crossing this beacon, likely carrying stolen goods they wish to sell later.
The enemy ship quickly reacts to your aggression, powering up their weapons in kind.
The ship doesn't ignores you and, once their drive finishes charging, jumps away.

destroyed.text: As you watch the Coalition vessel break apart, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: As their last crew falls, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_UNION_COALITION
event.text: You jump into this beacon to find a small Union shuttle barely managing to dodge the attacks from a R.U.E.S. ship. They desperately hail and bg you to help them.
The Coalition ship manages to disable the shuttle's shields, and just as they are about to destroy them you manage to catch their attention. Weapons up!
You shut off your communication device and prepare to jump, trying to forget this ever happened.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_UNION_COALITION_WIN
event.text: The Union crew are battered but not critically hurt. They thank you for your help and give an excess weapon they were planning to sell as a reward.

destroyed.text: The bright red shards of the Sentinel vessel fly off into the abyss as the mighty elite vessel comes to a halt.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Crystal ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: The last of crew of the mighty elite vessel falls to the ground, finally putting an end to this fight.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Crystal ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

destroyed.text: You've managed to take down a Cultivator and their ship, and now the remains are yours to scrap.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Vampweed ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've managed to take down the Cultivator's crew, and now their ship is yours to scrap.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Vampweed ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_VAMPWEED_CRYSTAL
event.text:
It seems this beacon was home of two distinct distress calls, one coming from Sentinel ship and another from a Cultivator. It seems the Federation has requested their aid in retaking the sector, but something must have happened to cause them to turn on each other. They seem to be locked into a deadly duel where the crystal's crew are all almost dead from the boarders while the vampweed ship is threatening to break apart at any moment from all the crystal shots it has taken.
Both ships have seen you already, so refusing to help either would lower your standing with both factions.
The Cultivator ship shambles away, attemping to avoid any stray shots, while the Sentinel ship turns its powerful elite weapons against you.
The Cultivator quickly beams back their crew, leaving any remaining crystals alive for the time being. Without a second thought they turn their weapons and crew towards you.
No matter the consequences, it's the far safer bet than fighting either one of those enemies.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_VAMPWEED_CRYSTAL_CRYSTAL_WON
event.text:
The almost broken Cultivator ship limps back into view after the fight is over, they hail after a short delay. "Federation renegade, never in a million years would I have thought that we would be actually happy to see one of you lot. We were called here to aid in your war effort, but those damn thick-headed crystals kept wanting to hunt down every god forseken pirate that came across our radar! We wanted them to understand that we were here on a very specific mission but they just wouldn't listen... We tried talking some sense into their thick skulls but... I fear I may have said things I now regret..."
The Cultivator sighs. "Well, my ship is almost destroyed and my crew are all gone... Mind if I hitch a ride on your ship for a while? I promise I will let my supperiors know of your deed here today."

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_VAMPWEED_CRYSTAL_VAMPWEED_WON
event.text:
The single remaining crew on the Sentinel ship hails you. "A Federation renegade... I see. I should have expected one of you to be here. This fight... shouldn't have happened. We were supposed to work together, but all those filthy pirates had to do was offer some scrap to those corrupt Vampweeds to look the otherway and get away scot-free every time! We have seen the damage those pirates can do with our own eyes! They single-handedly destroyed an elite Coalition ship in front of us! We have even heard rumors of them having entire fleets at their command now!"
The crystal sighs. "I suppose what's done is done... but my crew seem to have given up all their lives to protect me to no avail. My ship is far too damaged to be able to jump again without fear of it cracking at the seams... Would you be willing to let me serve on your ship until I can return to my home sector?"

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_REBEL_AUTO_BROKEN
event.text: You quickly find the source of the distress beacon, it seems an Innovation auto ship has been damaged enough to the point where it can't distinguish friend from foe. You could spend some time repairing it but chances are it will attack you if you repair its recognition hardware. Or you could take advantage of its malfunctioning sensors and get it to dispense supplyes to you.

The auto ship is repaired successfully without fixing its broken sensors after some time. "Technician offer. This vessel has been repaired to its full functionalities. Reward is due." The auto ship dispenses some supplies before jumping away.
After some time you manage to repair the auto ship successfully... maybe too successfully, as now that its sensors are online it quickly points their weapons at you.

As you attempt to repair the ship, you accidentally must have connected the wrong wires, causing a runaway explosion inside the ship. You are lucky none of your crew were hurt, but it was a close call. Unfortunately not much survived to be scrapped as the explosion almost completely obliterated the ship.

Damaged or not, Innovation auto ships are way too dangerous to be left operating, it's safer for everyone if we destroy it before it can harm anyone. The auto ship struggles to power its offensive measures, eventually damaging its own systems in the process. Still, even while damaged, Innovation ships are formidable foes.
Your Engineer knows exactly what to fix for the ship to give you some tempting supplies before initiating its self destruct protocols, that way it won't be a threat to anyone else.

Your Engi isn't used to working with rebel auto ships, and so isn't as efficient at repairing them as Engineers or Technicians, still it manages to repair the ship enough for it to give you some supplies. However midway through the exchange the auto ship detects you as an enemy and powers its offensive weapons to fight you.
The last thing you want to deal with is a malfunctioning auto ship. You keep your distance from it and prepare to jump.

destroyed.text: The mighty Federation ship crumbles to pieces. It's a shame such powerful technology had to be wasted like this...

event.text: The shuttle hails you again. "Thank you, Renegade. I will do my best to explain to my superiors what happened here today. I'm sure they will understand why you destroyed our ship. Feel free to scrap any pieces you might find; you earned it."

deadCrew.text: As the last Coalition soldier falls, you can't help but feel a strong sense of justice for avenging all the fallen. Hopefully losing these elite soldiers will slow down the Coalition's plans in this war.

event.text: The shuttle hails you again. "Renegade! You did it! I mean, of course you did, why did I ever doubt you? You not only took out some nasty Coalition soldiers, but you also stopped any plans they would have had with that ship. Please, have this weapon and as much scrap as we were carrying!".

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_FEDERATION_PIRATE
event.text: You find two ships in this beacon, a Unity shuttle and a Unity battleship, with the distress call coming from the shuttle. They hail you. "Renegade! You gotta help us! A group of Coalition soldiers took over our ship while we were sleeping! If they can infiltrate out ranks then the damage done could be immeasurable! Especially since we were also carrying a large supply of elite spore weapons! If you could keep the ship intact then we would greatly pay you everything we have aboard, but if you have to then we accept the consequences of having our old ship destroyed."
If there's any way you can help the defense of this sector, then that's an opportunity that you have to take. Even if that means fighting against Federation technology.
The shuttle's captain looks devastated. They open their mouth to speak but no words come out. They cut the video feed and you prepare to jump.

destroyed.text: The mysterious vessel is destroyed. You scrap its smooth white hull while the rainbow lights continue to flicker in and out of life.

deadCrew.text: The crew of the mysterious vessel is dead. You scrap its smooth white hull while the rainbow lights continue to flicker in and out of life.

event.text: To your surprise, you find another person stuffed into the storage of the alien ship. They appear to be under some strange trance that has now begun to slowly wear off. Once they're safe, they agree to join your crew.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_SIREN
event.text: You jump and find two ships, an EDC ship and a strange white ship with rainbow lights dancing across its hull. The militia ship hails you. "RENEGADE! PLEASE FOR THE LOVE OF GOD HELP US! THAT SHIP IS ABDUCTING ALL OF OUR CREW! WE TRIED FIGHTING THEM BUT JIM JUST PASSED OUT BEFORE HE COULD EVEN FIRE! I DON'T CARE WHAT YOU DO, JUST KILL IT PLEASE!"
You suddenly feel an immense headache coming up as soon as the enemy ship's guns point at you. You have no idea what the hell those things are, but you aren't letting them hurt anyone else!
The call soon turns silent, only a faint static being heard from the other end. After a few more minutes the strange ship jumps away.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_SIREN_WON
event.text: The EDC ship hails you. "Renegade! You actually did it! How the hell did you manage to resist the trance of those weird noodle monsters? I guess that doesn't matter, you are a hero in the EDC's eyes today! Please, take this augment as a token of gratitude, it's something every militia ship is given and greatly help us resist enemy ship suppress fire!"

destroyed.text: You've destroyed the Rebel Elite's ship: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet though, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've wiped out the crew of the Rebel Elite: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet though, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_GENERIC_REBEL
event.text: As soon as you land, you see a Union ship fleeing from an MFK Ace. The Union ship hails you and begs you to help them.

Before you can power your weapons, the MFK Ace manages to land a shot on the Union shuttle right as their shields were down from another volley. You aren't sure if they survived but you can't check right now as the Rebel points their guns at you.
It doesn't take long for the shuttle to be destroyed thanks to your inaction. To make things worse, the Rebel now points their weapons at you.

destroyed.text: As you watch the Coalition vessel break apart, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: As their last crew falls, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_GENERIC_COALITION
event.text: As soon as you land, you see a Union ship fleeing from an R.U.E.S. The Union ship hails you and begs you to help them.

Before you can power your weapons, R.U.E.S. manages to land a shot on the Union shuttle right as their shields were down from another volley. You aren't sure if they survived but you can't check right now as the Coalition ship points its guns at you.
It doesn't take long for the shuttle to be destroyed thanks to your inaction. To make things worse, the Coalition ship now points its weapons at you.

event.name: FR_DISTRESS_WARZONE_GENERIC_PIRATE
event.text: As soon as you land, you see a Union ship fleeing from an elite pirate. The Union ship hails you and begs you to help them.

Before you can power your weapons, the pirate manages to land a shot on the Union shuttle right as their shields were down from another volley. You aren't sure if they survived but you can't check right now as the pirate points their guns at you.
It doesn't take long for the shuttle to be destroyed thanks to your inaction. To make things worse, the pirate now points their weapons at you.
It seems the Union crew didn't survive the hit. With a heavy heart you scrap their ship.
The Union crew is mostly dead, save for a single crew. With their ship unoperable, they ask to join your crew.

The Union crew seem to have managed to fix their ship while the fight raged on. It's still quite damaged but it's intact enough to be able to reach the closest outpost. They thank you for your help and give you one of their guns plus some supplies.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_COALITION_RUES
event.text: The R.U.E.S. are only ever seen near either top secret mission or around the Coalition's home base of operations, even seeing one is something not all renegades can say they have done. They are cunning, ruthless and most of all, viscious. Unfortunately one has detected your presence in this beacon and is charging towards you!

The R.U.E.S. captain chuckles as they hail you. "Such a small and pitiful vessel you have. I'm surprised you weren't picked clean by pirates beforehand. Well, let me do what those weakligns couldn't!"

destroyed.text: No matter how imposing Night's ships are, they lack the discipline and training that other elites tend to get. Still, their dirty tactics clearly failed them today as their ship breaks apart at the seems.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Knight ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Knight crew are extremely resilient and are equipped with the best tech the Kleptocracy has to offer. Still, they die like any others.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Knight ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_COALITION_KNIGHT
event.text: It seems Nights must have been taking the Coalition's losses on this sector personally, as he sent a vessel of his personal elite soldiers flanked by a R.U.E.S. fleet! There's no way to avoid this fight either as the Knight is already barreling towards you before you can even react!

The Knight chuckles as they hail for just long enough to speak before shutting off the call. "It sssseemssss you made a... grave misssstake today, renegade." You aren't sure if that was a pun or not, but you don't have the time to ponder that as the enemy is already firing in your general direction!

destroyed.text: Even the Hive's strongest ships have their limits, and it seems the enemy Bishop has finally reached theirs. With one last crack the ship explodes into pieces.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Bishop ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Mantis Bishops are some of the best warriors the multiverse has to offer, they capture the pwerfect mix of speed and power. No matter how mighty, though, all warriors must fall one day.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Bishop ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_COALITION_BISHOP
event.text: If there's a battle going on, you can bet your bottom scrap that a Mantis isn't too far behind to join it. Bishops are even more willing and ready to serve their Queen, no matter where and no matter when. Case in point, the Bishop ship charging at you at speeds too high for you to escape easily.
The Bishop hails you. "Renegade! Queen Leikonpliiinakra requested sector captured! Nothing stands in way your majesty's plans! Fight well and best warriors wins!"

destroyed.text: While Paladin ships might seem indestructable, they can still be cracked open with enough force. There's few people who have managed to destroy a Paladin's hull and lived to tell the tale. Now you are one of them.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Paladin ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Just like their ships, Paladins are the toughest and most resistant crew you can ever come across in the multiverse. Some might even think they are immortal given how much punishment they can take without even flinching, and yet here you stand with the enemy ship devoid of life.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Paladin ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_COALITION_PALADIN
event.text: The Theocracy only brings out their Paladin ships when their will needs to be enforced through extreme measures. They crush both the enemy's resistance fighters and their hopes at the same time. They're no slouches in the speed department either, as you can clearly see from the Rock ship barreling towards you at a high enough speed to make any escape impossible.

The Paladin hails you. "Bah, just another filty Federation bug that needs squashing. And here I thought we had finally found a worthy challange in this sector of weaklings. You will know the wrath of the Inquisition here today, heretic!"

destroyed.text: The Amperes are trained explicitly by the Coalition itself, far deadlier than normal Leeches. By destroying this ship, you've probably crippled a good percentage of the Republic's only able soldiers.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Leech ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: The Amperes are trained explicitly by the Coalition itself, and are far deadlier than normal Leeches. By killing all their crew, you've probably crippled a good percentage of the Republic's only able soldiers.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Leech ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_COALITION_AMPERE
event.text: The Coalition must be getting desperate if they are bringing their Amperes to the fight. While their ships are quite formidable, their crew aren't anywhere near experienced enough to handle the same heat as other elites of the faction. Still, that doesn't mean they don't occasionally do something right, like catching you off guard right now and forcing a fight.
The Ampere hails you. "I'm tired of having to avoid you Renegades everytime we need to scout a beacon! Time for you to finally fear our alliance!" The ship's weapons power up to engage you.

destroyed.text: You've destroyed the Rebel Elite's ship: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet though, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've wiped out the crew of the Rebel Elite: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet though, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_REBEL_MFK
event.text: The Rebellion's assault on this sector is still at full force, even if they also need to defend it from the Coalition. No expense is spared on making absolutely sure the sector is theirs. This seems to be mainly in the form of flooding the sector with MFK Aces. Speaking of which, one seems to have already found you. You're cornered you before you can even charge your jump.
The MFK Ace hails you. "Federation scum! This sector is ours now and there's nothing you or those filthy aliens can do to stop our plans! For the Rebellion!"

destroyed.text: You've defeated a technician vessel, impressively. Though they may simply be menial workers in the eyes of Wentworth Innovations, their ships are considerably well-armed, and successfully defeating one in combat is no small feat.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Technician ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've killed all of the technician vessel's crew, impressively. Though they may simply be menial workers in the eyes of Wentworth Innovations, their crew's drones are surprisingly competetent in combat, and successfully defeating one is no small feat.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Technician ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_REBEL_TECHNICIAN
event.text: The Rebellion rarely sends their Field Technicians into combat, especially in disputed sectors. Their combat abilities are simply not on par with their MFK Aces or even elite Rebels. However, it seems the Rebellion is desperate enough for control of this sector to send a Technician ship to take you out.

The Field Technician hails you. "I apologise for what we need to do, but unfortunately we have been given very firm instructions to make sure you won't stop any plans our bosses have. Wentowrth Innovations would appreciate your cooperation if you just depowered your systems and made our life easier."
It seems the cargo of the Innovation Auto ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional sentry weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_REBEL_ESTATE
event.text: Innovation auto ships are some of the finest auto ships that the Rebellion has to offer, with the only possible exception being a super secret auto class that no one has ever seen firsthand and is only vaguely alluded to in some confidential Rebel papers. Built by the most skilled Wentworth Field Technicians, these elite ships can hold their own even against powerful battleships. Hopefully you are prepared to fight them since one is charging straight at you!
The Innovation Auto hails you. "Federation ship. You have been scanned and deemed a threat to our future operations. There will be no mercy. Death to the Federation! For the Rebellion!"

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_UNITY
event.text: The Federation has been on their back foot ever since the Rebels first invaded the sector. The subsequent invasion of the Coalition only stoked the flames of chaos in their already disorganised defences. It seems that the stress of the whole situation is finally getting to some of the soldiers, as a Unity vessel just mistook you for a hired Rebel mercenary.

The Human soldier in charge of the ship hails you. "The Rebels are hiring Renegades now?! Great, that's just freaking great! Hear me, mercenary scum, we will not kowtow to neither the Rebels, nor the Coalition, nor whatever other faction tries to break us! Our unity won't be so easily broken!"

destroyed.text: The Federation has precious few stealth ships in service, and eliminating one is a major blow to their special operations. But your mission is more important, and you waste little time in salvaging the remains.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the stealth ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Federation stealth ships have some of the best soldiers the Federation has to offer, and so what you have done here today will unfortunately cause severe repercussions for future plans of the Federation.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the stealth ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_STEALTH
event.text: You are surprised to see a Federation stealth ship out in the open like this. It seems your arrival in this beacon has disrupted whatever the captain of the ship was planning, because they are now heading towards you with their weapons online and cloaking system charged!

The captain of the stealth ship hails you. "You! Of course command would send some of you renegade Morons to 'assist' with retaking this sector! We told them over and over that we didn't need any help whatsoever! If anything, you are an active liability! So screw it! If command won't listen to reason, then I will make sure you won't set us back any more than your presence already has!"

destroyed.text: The EDC is one of the few forces capable of defending civilian sectors, and they aren't exactly common. The loss of this ship will certainly have negative effects on the Militia.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Militia ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: The EDC soldiers might not have the most high-grade training when compared to elite Federation personnel, but they can still put up one hell of a fight when push comes to shove.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Militia ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_EDC
event.text: The EDC or 'Elite Defence Cadre' is the best of the best that the militia has to offer. Under the command of Senator Wright, the EDC might be underequipped when compared to the Federation's Unity ships, but that doesn't mean they should be understimated. You attempt to hail the nearby EDC ship but all you get is a warning shot in your general direction.

The captain of the EDC ship hails you. "Bah! More reckless Renegades putting their noses where they don't belong! Do you think we will ever forgive your type after the Rusting Hulks incident? Not a chance! Weapons up, criminal!"

destroyed.text: Harmony ships are the Harmony's main line of defense against outside threats. The destruction of this ship today will surely leave them open to harsher attacks.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Engi ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional drone, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: With the death of the Engi Defenders there are now fewer ships to protect the Harmony from true threats, but you can't run away from every fight. You salvage the remains of the ship.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Engi ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional drone, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_HARMONY
event.text: The Federation is clearly not going down quietly in this sector, and to prove it they've even received the support of Harmony elite ships. It's strange to see these ships out here, given the fact that Engi that stray too far from their links tend to end up needing to be recycled. Maybe that has something to do with this specific Harmony ship deeming you a threat as it advances towards you.
The Defender hails you. "Ship scanned. Deemed to be a: Renegade. Risk for harmful unplanned actions = 98%. Risk exceeds acceptable parameters. Solution: Eliminate threat."

destroyed.text: So much for peacekeeping. The Ministry struggles to keep its Peacekeeper task force up to par with the chaotic threats in the Multiverse, and you destroying their ships certainly won't help that.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Zoltan ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Peacekeepers are the most strict and disciplined soldiers in the whole Federation. They might not be the most durable or the most powerful, but what they lack in specialization they more than make up for in versitility.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Zoltan ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_PEACE_KEEPER
event.text: The Ministry didn't waste any time in helping the Federation retake this sector, sending out waves upon waves of their best soldiers for the war effort, although it's clear their intentions are far from autruistic. If they help the Federation retake the sector, the Ministry will be able to claim jurisdiction and control over it, since their presence in the sector is the heaviest out of all Federation allies. It seems like the Ministry isn't going to wait around for the war to be finished before securing the sector, as one of their Peacekeeper ships has cornered you and is powering on their weapons.

The Peacekeeper hails you. "Renegade, you are under arrest for trespassing into a sector labled as an 'Active Warzone' by the Ministry, as described under the Military Cooperation Act 66-T, Section 11-A. Do not resist or risk immediate execution."

destroyed.text: You've managed to take down a Praetor and their ship, and now the remains are yours to scrap.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Orchid ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Praetors aren't easily replaceble, and the Gathering have enough enemies as is. Hopefully the loss of these highly-trained soldiers won't spell doom for the Gathering in the long run.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Orchid ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_PRAETOR
event.text: While generally a peaceful race, the Floral Orchids are no strangers to war. Their elite ships have powerful kernel weapons that can easily swing the tide of battle by themselves. Unfortunately, either by a miscommunication or enemy deception, this Praetor has deemed you the threat they were assigned to take out!

The Praetor hails you. "Renegade, I hope you don't take this personally, but high command has ordered you to be removed from the frontlines by force. We'd rather not escalate this situation but if you resist we will be forced to neutralize you."

event.name: FR_ELITE_WARZONE_PIRATE
event.text: The war for this sector has truly unearthed the true threat that the unaffiliated pirates can pose. Their use of highly illigal and dangerous weapons combined with their truly reckless abandon when it comes to picking their targets makes them a threat that should be taken as seriously as the MFK Aces or R.U.E.S.. It seems you are now one of their targets, as an elite pirate has caught you off guard!

The pirate hails you "My my, what a fancy ship you got there. I'm sure it would fetch a ton of scrap in the right hands. And then, with that kinda money, I could finally earn Purple Beard's admiration! Time to die, Renegade!"

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GATLING
event.text:
As soon as you arrive you notice a huge crowd of Federation ships stationed around a Unity station. As you cycle through the Federation Military bands, you find the one that all the ships seem to be using to communicate.
There seems to be a meeting going on between a human soldier, a Defender Engi, a Peace-keeper Zoltan and what seems to be a high-ranking Federation official. The soldier is the first to speak. "You must be MAD, Lieutenant Kely! We don't have the equipment to fight off their elites! We would all be obliterated in seconds! Even if Luminus was able to gather a few of his Ministry friends to help us, a single MFK Ace would blast us back to the Stone Ages!"
The Lieutenant sighs, her short faded green-dyed hair almost fully obscured by her helmet. "I understand your concerns, Officer Jan, but we are running out of options fast. This package is *essential*. We can't allow either the Rebels or the Coalition to get their hands on the full machine, no matter the cost." Her voice is tired but she maintains her resolve.
The Defender engi speaks, their voice changing pitch every other sentence. "Affirmative. However, risk of failure exceeds safe parameters. Probability of total failure = almost guaranteed." The Peacekeeper groans. "Look, can we speed this up? If we are about to die anyways then we might as well just get it over with."

Kely looks at you, clearly startled by your interjection. "Ah, you must be the Renegade we hired for this reality. I suppose you are better-equipped than any of us at the moment. Very well, I will send you the coordinates to the first piece. There are two in total; once you have both of them, you will need to deliver them to my superiors. Don't worry about the specifics; once you get the first piece, you will be directed to the other one."
The peace-keeper stares at you for a moment. "If you want to go on this mission then the least we can do is offer you something to help you fight off whatever foes you come across. We can't afford to give you everything but feel free to pick any of our offers."

The Lieutenant sighs. "I'm sorry, but we don't have anyone that we can spare. Is there anything else you need?" However, before you can answer, Officer Jan gets up from his chair. "Bullshit, I'm still here and I'll be damned if I just stand around waiting for you to be destroyed by a roaming pirate!" Jan beams himself aboard your ship, much to the annoyance of Kely, who sighs exasperatedly. "Very well then... Let me give you the coordinates."

The Lieutenant nods "We received a shipment of beam weapons recently. We have too many of them for the few Unity ships that can properly use them." She beams the beam weapon into your ship and then hands you the coordinated.

The Engi perks up. "This unit's vessel has been outfitted with an excessive amount of combat drones. Surplus equipment raises chances for pirate attacks by: 50%." The Engi beams a combat drone aboard your ship while Kely gives you the coordinates.

The Zoltan smiles. "I see you are a person of good taste, Renegade. The art of combat augments is one that has been lost recently. I make and sell augments as a hobby, but I can spare one for you as thanks for getting us out of this pickle." The Zoltan transfers the augment to you while Kely sends over the coordinates.

choice.text: Ignore the conversation and move on.

event.text: As dire as things are, your mission is as important as theirs. You charge your FTL and prepare to jump.

destroyed.text: The massive MFK Ace ship cracks apart as its artillery attempts to shoot one last volley, only for its missiles to jam, causing a huge explosion in the remaining front of the ship.

deadCrew.text: The MFK Aces do their best to hold on to their powerful vessel but at some point it becomes clear which side will be winning. To your surprise, all the remaining MFK Aces get into escape pods and flee, leaving the remaining soldiers alone to deal with your angry crew. It isn't long before they either surrender or die protecting the ship.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GATLING_PART_1

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: Location of the first part of the machine.
beaconType.visitedTooltip: Explored location. Nothing left of interest.

event.text:
You reach the coordinates for the first half of whatever machine Lieutenant Kely was so worried about securing. To your surprise you not only find an MFK Ace ship here, but a rebel cruiser painted in MFK Ace colors! It seems this cruiser destroyed a Unity ship before you arrived and was about to collect a strangely reinforced metal crate. That must be the package you are after!
They hail you. "Hah! I was told some Federation clown would come and try stop us or save that poor sap that we just destroyed. Frankly, I thought they would have at least sent a fleet against us. What is this pathetic excuse for a ship?" The Rebel laughs before her tone gets more serious. "Alright, Feddy boy, let me be crystal clear here. You either walk away and pretend you never saw us, or we will blast your ship to atoms, got it?"

choice.text: We won't back down! We need that package!

event.text: You power on your weapons while the enemy ship does the same. This won't be pretty, but you can't risk losing that package!

choice.text: (Vance Prime) "Halt, soldier! I order you back away from the wreck and allow us to operate in peace!"

event.text: The captain blinks, their eyes almost bulging out of their socket. "V-Vance Prime?! I-I'm so sorry sir! W-We will leave you be at once!" The Rebel stutters before jumping away.

choice.text: Do as they say and back away.

event.text: The Rebel captain laughs before taking the metal box and jumping away. You pray whatever was inside of it doesn't end up spelling the doom of the Federation...

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GATLING_PART_1_WIN
event.text: With the Rebel ship dealt with, you are free to secure the crate. As you bring it aboard your ship, you notice a place for you to insert your Federation ID into. After doing so, the crate verifies you as a member of the Federation and uploads the next set of coordinates into your ship's map system. You decide to open the crate, only to find what seems to be parts of a gun, one that seems to have a shallow resemblence to the Vulcan, just much bigger. It's currently unusable though, since the other half is necessary for the gun to actually fire.
