destroyed.text: The mighty R.U.E.S. cruiser splits in half. Pheromones leak uncontrolably even as the slug gel tries to keep most of the ship together. Eventually, even the gel isn't enough to keep the ship together and it collapses.

deadCrew.text: The last to fall is unsurprisingly the Paladin, their extremely durable body making them seem invincible... almost. There's no escape attempt this time; the crew goes down with the ship.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GATLING_PART_2

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: Location of the second part of the machine.
beaconType.visitedTooltip: Explored location. Nothing left of interest.

event.text:
You reach the coordinates for the second half of this strange weapon only to find another Unity ship, this time a battleship, intact but completely inert. You also see a massive R.U.E.S. cruiser, armed and ready to engage with you. However, instead of attacking right away, they hail you.
"Oh my goodnesssss! Issss that a little Federation sssship wanting to 'avenge' their dead friendssss?!" The Knight captain burst out laughing. "That'ssss the cutesssst thing I have sssseen all day! You are a funny persssson, Federation renegade. I like funny people. You can leave now, unlesssss you want to end up like your friendssss over there."

choice.text: We will make you pay for what you did!

event.text: The slug chuckles before their expression hardens. "Fine, your funeral."

choice.text: We don't have the first half of the gun. Getting the second half would be pointless without it.

event.text:
"Noble warriors! This is your Queen speaking! I demand you vacate this beacon immediately!"
The Knight captain freezes. This is the first time you have ever seen a Knight completely out of their element. "O-Of coursssse, Your Highnesssss! We will leave right away!" The captain does as ordered and jumps away, giving you free acess to the wreck.

"Hey! SSSSoldier! Take your fancy sssship and get the hell out of here!"
The Knight captain freezes before nodding violently. "O-Of coursssse, Your Highnesssss! We will leave right away!" The captain does as ordered and jumps away, giving you free acess to the wreck.

"Excuse me, but I'm Tyrdeo, the President of the Republic. I'm sure you are aware that the Coalition soldiers cannot threaten their leaders without severe consequences. If you were to leave, I would be willing to 'forget' this little event."
The Knight captain freezes before gulping and nodding slowly. "O-Of coursssse, Your Excellency. We will leave right away." The captain does as ordered and jumps away, giving you free acess to the wreck.

choice.text: Do as they say and back away.

event.text: The captain grins 'ear' to 'ear' before their crew takes a metal crate from inside the empty Federation battleship and jumps away without another word.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GATLING_PART_2_WIN
event.text: With the R.U.E.S. Cruiser out of your way, you are free to investigate the Federation battleship and recover the other half of the strange weapon. You find a metal box almost identical to the first one. You bring it aboard and unlock it the same way you did with the other half. The verification process is slow but it eventually unlocks and gives you the final coordinates, pointing to a nearby Federation outpost. Whoever is in there will be able to re-assemble whatever super weapon this thing is.

destroyed.text: It's a shame to have to destroy a cruiser that certainly must have had quite the story, but in life-or-death scenarios, history doesn't matter as much as you staying alive.

deadCrew.text: The trail of misery these pirates must have left in their wake is uncountable. How many hundreds of ships did they rob, de-crew, or destroy before they met their ends at your hands? You have no idea, but at least they can't hurt anyone else anymore.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GATLING_PART_FINAL

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: Location of the Federation outpost needed to fix the gun.
beaconType.visitedTooltip: Explored location. Nothing left of interest.

event.text:
As you reach the Federation outpost given by the coordinates, you are shocked by what you see: a Unity Kestrel that has been somehow commandeered by pirates! Unity Kestrels are rare enough to see outside of Universe 0's mega shipyards, but to see one fall prey to unaffiliated pirates is a once-in-a-lifetime event.
Unfortunately, it doesn't seem like the Kestrel is alone, as you can see a few pirate fleets attacking the few remaining Unity fleet ships. If you were to guess, another faction must have attacked this outpost before the pirates because there's no way unaffiliated pirates could overpower a well-defended Unity outpost.
You are hailed by the outpost's captain. "A Renegade? What are you doing here?" You explain to the captain of the outpost about your mission to deliver the weapon parts. She seems relieved once she finally realizes what you are talking about. "Oh thank the stars the gun didn't fall prey to the Rebels or the Coalition! Look, we will gladly let you keep the gun once it's repaired, but please help us first! We think the leader of this attack is in the Kestrel, since it's where most of the radio waves are coming from. Please take them out by whatever means necessary!"

You are suddenly hailed by the Kestrel as you aproach it. On screen is a man who is likely the captain of the ship. "Ohohoho! Another challanger joins the ring, eh? Well if today isn't my lucky day! I always wanted to fight a Renagade! Now come! Let's battle this out, cruiser on cruiser!"
Before the battle can start, however, you see a Unity ship, a Peace-Keeping ship, a Federation ship, and a Harmony ship all arrive together. You are hailed by the Unity ship and on screen is Kely! "Renegade! Thank goodness you are OK! Our station was unfortunately destroyed by a fleet of MFK Aces, but we all managed to escape. We heard of a pirate attack and came here to try and help them but it seems our paths crossed once more. We will help defend the outpost; focus on fighting off the Kestrel!"

choice.text: (Purple Beard) Purple Beard wants to hail the pirates.

event.text:
"Hear me!... If you value... your lives... I suggest... you stand down... and leave this place! All of you!"
There's silence on your comms for several seconds before the captain of the Kestrel replies, their voice shakey and unsure. "Oh... shit... Fellas! Everyone scram!" The comm systems erupt with the sounds of people panicking, and just like that every pirate has left the area without saying another word. You look at Purple Beard, who seems proud of what he has just done, for once.

choice.text: This gun is too important to back off now.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GATLING_PART_FINAL_WIN_PURPLE_BEARD
event.text:
It doesn't take long for the last few remaining pirates to leave the scene. The few that stay to fight are quickly captured or eliminated.
You are hailed by the outpost. "Renegade! I-I can't believe my eyes! They all scattered! I thought most of them would have stayed and fought; they clearly had the numbers advantage! But I guess this shows that you can never fully predict what a pirate will do. We normally don't give this much away for good deeds, but I think you earned some extra special compensation today!"

choice.text: We were glad to help, but what about the gun?

event.text:
"O-Oh! Right right right! Sorry, I almost forgot! Please, come inside our outpost! We will fix up that gun and, as a gesture of good will, you can keep it and use it as you see fit!" You dock your ship on the outpost alongside Kely and her friends. You two meet in the outpost and talk while your gun is assembled. Once it's finished, Kely herself offers to install the gun on your ship; apparently, she used to be a engineer before the war.
Once everything is setup, you bid farewell to the outpost and return to your ship. You have a feeling the word of your deeds here will spread far and wide.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_FEDERATION_GATLING_PART_FINAL_WIN
event.text:
As soon as the Kestrel falls, all the pirate ships suddenly stop for a few seconds. For a moment you worry that they will try to avenge their leader, but thankfully instead they all scatter, problably seeing the way the fight is going to end.
You are hailed by the outpost. "Renegade! I-I can't believe my eyes! They all scattered! I mean, I thought they would be easier to defeat without their leader but I wouldn't have ever guessed that they would disband on the spot! Shows you that you can never trust a pirate to have your back. We normally don't give this much away for good deeds, but I think you earned some extra special compensation today!"

choice.text: We were glad to help, what about the gun though?

event.text:
"O-Oh! Right right right! Sorry, I almost forgot! Please, come inside our outpost! We will fix up that gun and, as a gesture of good will, you can keep it and use it as you see fit!" You dock your ship on the outpost alongside Kely and her friends. You two meet in the outpost and talk while your gun is assembled. Once it's finished, Kely herself offers to install the gun on your ship; apparently, she used to be a engineer before the war.
Once everything is setup, you bid farewell to the outpost and return to your ship. You have a feeling the word of your deeds here will spread far and wide.

destroyed.text: You've destroyed the Rebel Elite's ship: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet though, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've wiped out the crew of the Rebel Elite: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet though, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_REBEL_ESCAPE
event.text:
It seems you aren't alone on this beacon, as an MFK Ace ship is parked close by. Strangely, their weapons remain offline though they seem to spot you. Instead of a fight, they hail you. "Renegade! I'm so glad you were the one that found me and not some Coalition dog or strict Federation soldier. Look, let's not waste your time here and get straight to the point. I do not want to be here and fight this endless war for someone I've never met nor care about. I want O U T, do you get me? We saw a few MFK Ace pirates when fighting around in this sector, and we believe that a pirate's life might be just what we need."
The rebel clears their throat before speaking again. "This ship and crew... well, they became my new home after we left Universe 1, and I can't bear to leave them behind. So I've been scouting ahead, asking the right people the right questions, and I found a particular beacon that doesn't have any Rebel presence on it. If you could bring us there undetected then we can find a way to exit this sector without command even realizing we were gone. We won't need all the guns we got from the ships we destroyed here once we are free, so we plan to compensate you really well once this is over. So... what do you say, renegade?"

The MFK grins and nods. "Good choice, Renegade. We will follow you but we will be about a jump behind so no one sees us working together. It wouldn't look good on your resume if your 'friends' saw you working with the 'enemy'. Good luck, Renegade. See you at the beacon... hopefully."
The MFK Ace growls and shuts down the call before their weapons finally come to life.

destroyed.text: Unity ships aren't easy to replace, and with the Federation already on the back foot of the war, this loss will end up having much worse consequences than expected.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Federation ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Eliminating allied Federation personnel, especially Elite ones, is highly contrary to your mission, but that doesn't mean you're beyond salvaging the remains.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Elite Federation ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

destroyed.text: You've destroyed the Rebel Elite's ship: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've wiped out the crew of the Rebel Elite: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_REBEL_ESCAPE_EXIT

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: The MFK Ace wants help leaving the sector. They promised a big reward if you can help them.

event.text: You reach the beacon the Rebel pointed to only to find it being host to a huge skirmish between the Federation and MFK Aces. You attempt to hail the ship you were leading but it seems you were too late as they jump right after you land. At first you hope no one else noticed you or the Rebel but a Unity ship quickly notices you two. "Rebel! This is the end of the line for you! Come on Renegade, let's kill this fascist!" The Unity ship moves to attack the Rebel, leaving you with only two choices... It seems the only ship that noticed you two was the Unity ship, meaning you can either betray the rebels or destroy the Federation ship.

choice.text: (Tully) Pretend the MFK Ace is a spy.

event.text:
"Brave Federation soldier! This Rebel ship is one of our best spies! Power down your weapons and allow them to leave the area!"
The Unity captain is a bit confused at first but quickly grins and nods. "Ooooh I see what you mean! Great plan, Admiral! We will go back to kicking some Rebel ass. You do whatever you feel like you have to, we won't get on your way!"

With the Unity ship out of your hide, the way is clear for the Rebel to escape. They hail you. "Renegade! You didn't tell us that you had Admiral Freaking Tully aboard your ship! How the hell did you manage that?! Anyways, I belive it's time that we part ways. Here's the weapon as promised. Also, while we were following you, one of my best soldiers asked if they could join your crew. It seems piracy isn't the life they want, and I would rest easier knowing they can be happy in the Federation. Have a good life Renegade, and thank you."

choice.text: Hold your end of the bargain and defend the MFK Ace by attacking the Federation ship.

event.text: You use the Unity ship's distraction to your advantage and manage to hit their communication systems before they could react. Hopefully this will prevent them from calling backup. The MFK Ace ship stays away from the fight, hoping to blend in with the wrecked ships.

choice.text: Betray the Rebel and attack them alongside the Unity ship.

event.text: The MFK Ace is visibly betrayed, their shock quickly turning to anger as they turn on their weapons at max power. The Unity ship prepares to send a backup signal to the nearby fleet, but the MFK decides to do the same.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_REBEL_ESCAPE_EXIT_FEDERATION_WIN
event.text: With the Unity ship down, the way is clear for the Rebel to escape. They hail you. "Renegade! You are alive! I mean- Of course you are! Your type doesn't go down easy huh? Anyways, I belive it's time that we part ways. Here's the weapon as promised. Also, while we were following you, one of my best soldiers asked if they could join your crew. It seems piracy isn't the life they want, and I would rest easier knowing they can be happy in the Federation. Have a good life Renegade, and thank you."

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_REBEL_ESCAPE_EXIT_REBEL_WIN
event.text: It pains you to have to dispose to someone you promised to protect, but you couldn't risk becoming an enemy of the Federation, not in this hell hole. The Unity ship hails you. "Good fighting, renegade! Command will be proud of what we acomplished here today! Here, we had quite the surplus of weapons from all the ships we scrapped; I'm sure you can find a good use for this."

gotaway.text: Whatever secrets that ship had will remain a mystery. All you hope is that this loss won't spell the end to the Federation's efforts on this sector...

destroyed.text: The strange ship's hull cracks and splinters open, leaking copious amounts of nebula gas as it collapses. It seems they were the reason for this out-of-place nebula cloud in the sector. You manage to find a weapon that survived the ship's destruction but whatever information it had is gone for good.

deadCrew.text: The enemy crew seemed weirdly on edge as they fought you; even the slug didn't seem keen on quips or insults. Whatever mission they were on must have been quite important.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_COALITION_CEALAFORMER
event.text:
You wonder why there's a singular beacon in the whole sector that's inside an out-of-place nebula. According to your database, this sector wasn't meant to even have any nebula-covered beacons. You start exploring this strange place when you see an even stranger R.U.E.S. ship in the distance. The nebula is too thick to make out any details but the silhouette isn't one from a ship you recognise.
You approach the ship to get a better look at it, but before you can hail it, the ship seems to notice you and starts backing away. It isn't long before its FTL drive starts charging.

While the ship might act evasive, that doesn't mean it's invulnerable. When their crew realizes they won't be able to escape so easily, the ship's weapons power on, though their FTL drive doesn't stop charging. If you want whatever secrets it has you need to dispose of their crew before they run away!

choice.text: (Leikonpliiinakra) Leikonpliiinakra stays silent and refuses to appear on the feed.
(Nights) You tried to ask Nights but he's nowhere to be found.
(Tyrdeo) Tyrdeo runs away from the room the moment the strange ship appears in your radar and hides in his cabin, refusing to get out until you leave the beacon.

event.text: Whatever that ship is, it doesn't want any trouble with you, so you don't want any trouble with it either.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_COALITION_CEALAFORMER_WIN
event.text: As the ship goes empty, you take the opportunity to give the strange vessel a thorough search. After a while you find the captain's quarters and inside are the coordinates to a hidden beacon in the sector, one that isn't in any maps you know about. You punch the coordinates into your ship's navigation systems and prepare to leave.

destroyed.text: You've destroyed the odd auto ship. Its powerful weapons felt like something out of this world, but it seems nothing can stop you now.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the strange auto ship survived the fight. Inside is a highly advanced weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_COALITION_CEALAFORMER_RUINS

beaconType.unvisitedTooltip: The location of the coordinates from the strange ship.

event.text: As you reach the strange unmarked beacon, you see a whole fleet of destroyed ships streching as far as your eyes can see. They all look completely foreign to anything you've come across before, with their strange construction style and odd dark materials. Soon, however, you realize you aren't the only thing active in this graveyard of ships, as a strange white and pink auto ship emerges from inside one of the fleet ships. It doesn't attack you yet, but it doesn't hail you either.

choice.text: Attack ther ship! Whatever's inside those broken ships must be worth protecting!

event.text: The ship's lights shine brightly, almost blinding you, before it powers an array of highly futuristic-looking equipment. Whatever this ship is, it isn't messing around!

choice.text: Avoid the strange ship and hope it doesn't attack you.

event.text: You slowly back away from the auto ship, and after a tense few minutes, the other ship backs away too and flies back inside the fleet.

event.name: FR_QUEST_WARZONE_COALITION_CEALAFORMER_RUINS_WIN
event.text: With the auto ship disposed of, you are free to explore this strange place for as much time as you have before the fleet closes in. You spend a few hours exploring all acessible fleet ships, and in the end you find a single ship with an untouched cargo bay.

Inside the cargo bay you find 3 pedestals, one with a powerful looking gun, one with a extremely advanced looking drone, and another with some kind of augment installer that looks advanced beyond anything you have ever seen before.
At first you think you can take all 3, but right before you take the gun you see a few lasers protecting it and the other three pedestals. You can take one for sure, but the other two will at best be locked out of your reach or at worst be destroyed.

You ready yourself before quickly snatching the gun and then running back to your ship as quickly as possible. It seems you were right; the other options were destroyed, but so was the rest of the ship! Thankfully, you managed to escape with your prize intact. Seeing no other ship with an acessible cargo bay you decide it's time to go back to your mission.

You ready yourself before quickly snatching the drone and then running back to your ship as quickly as possible. It seems you were right; the other options were destroyed, but so was the rest of the ship! Thankfully, you managed to escape with your prize intact. Seeing no other ship with an acessible cargo bay you decide it's time to go back to your mission.

You ready yourself before quickly snatching the augment and then running back to your ship as quickly as possible. It seems you were right; the other options were destroyed, but so was the rest of the ship! Thankfully, you managed to escape with your prize intact. Seeing no other ship with an acessible cargo bay you decide it's time to go back to your mission.

destroyed.text: You've destroyed the Rebel Elite's ship: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: You've wiped out the crew of the Rebel Elite: no easy task. It's not enough to impact the fleet, but it's still a great victory.

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Rebel ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_REBEL_FANATIC
event.text: You've only just arrived at the beacon when a MFK Ace ship immediately rushes towards you, hailing you. "Federation scum! You will pay for all you have done to slow down humanity's progress and status! Forcing us to live alongside alien scum as if we were equal! But no more! The Rebellion will free us all from the chains of oppression and bring about a future fit for our kind!"

choice.text: Have you even met any aliens before?

event.text: Your words don't even register to the MFK Ace. They cut the feed and attempt to attack you while your weapons are offline. Thankfully the shot misses, but their message is clear: you can't avoid this fight!
It seems the cargo of the Innovation Auto ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional sentry weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_REBEL_AUTO_HACK
event.text: Before you can even react, all of your subsystems are instantly hacked, with only piloting remaining barely functional. You quickly find the culprit, a Innovation Saboteur who identified you as a threat and is moving in to engage.
The Anti-Virus works its magic and counters the digital assault without any problems.
The Innovation ship's virus is much harder to purge than you expected, and you have to overclock the hacking system. Your systems return to normal, but your hacking has taken a huge loss.

destroyed.text: As you watch the Coalition vessel break apart, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: As their last crew falls, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_COALITION_BOARDING
event.text: You, at first, find yourself alone near the unpopulated moon on this beacon. That is, until a R.U.E.S. ship comes out of hiding, guns blazing!

The ship hails you. A Saboteur appears on screen and chuckles. "Come on, Renegade, did you really think this ship was all we had on this beacon?" The slug snickers and then grins. "Oh nonono, in fact, I guess you can say you've fallen into our little... trap." the slug bursts out laughing as your boarder alarms blare! You also detect more teleporter signatures flaring up on the nearby moon; they must be using the R.U.E.S. ship as a way to track your ship's location!

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: More elite Coalition soldiers will board your ship! End the fight with the enemy ship to stop them!

event.text: Leikonpliiinakra doesn't need to even say a word before the enemy ship's weapons power down. "...sssshit..." The Saboteur mutters before sending a gun and some resouces as an apology.
Nights doesn't need to even say a word before the enemy ship's weapons power down. "...sssshit..." The Saboteur mutters before sending a gun and some resouces as an apology.
Tyrdeo doesn't need to even say a word before the enemy ship's weapons power down. "...sssshit..." The Saboteur mutters before sending a gun and some resouces as an apology.

event.name: FR_WARZONE_COALITION_BOARDING_WAVE
event.text: Like clockwork another wave of Coalition elites board your ship! If you want to end this endless tide of death then you must dispose of the enemy ship!

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_COALITION_BOARDING_WIN
event.text: As the R.U.E.S. ship finally falls, your alarms go silent and all the detected teleporter signatures vanish from your sensors. You at least now have the breathing room to do something aboard your ship and double check where you fought the ship for any extra resouces you might have missed..

destroyed.text: As you watch the Coalition vessel break apart, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: As their last crew falls, you reflect on what a stupid acronym "R.U.E.S." is. Like seriously, who even came up with that?

event.text: It seems that the cargo of the Elite Coalition ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_COALITION_ASTEROID
event.text: While uncommon, there are still a few asteroid belts in this sector. The Federation, Rebellion, and Coalition avoid them as places for skirmishes, as it turns combat into something a lot more hectic for all parties involved. Sometimes, though, individual ships still tactically use them for one-on-one fights. Case in point, the R.U.E.S. ship here that's advancing towards you.

The ship hails you. They seem to have an Ampere as their captain. "God dammit, I knew even the asteroid belts weren't safe! You know what? I'm tired of having to avoid you renegades everytime we need to scout a beacon! Time for you to finally fear our alliance!" the leech screams before powering on the ship's weapons.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_PIRATE_SHAKEDOWN
event.text: Elite pirates have become a plague on this sector, swarming civilian and defenseless ships alike and shaking them down for everything they have. Case in point, the elite pirate that seems to be preying on weaker ships at this beacon. The captain grins widely. "Well well well, what do we have here? A small Renegade all alone, without their nosey Federation friends around to help, hm? Well, I can let you through unharmed... for a price."

choice.text: We refuse to pay the fee, fight your way through!

event.text: You mute the ship and prepare for combat. However, before you can properly start the fight, the pirate activates a combat augment, heavily limiting your engines!

triggeredEventBox.tooltip: You've been affected by an engine jammer!

event.text: The pirate's eyes widen before they chuckle, clearly unconfortable. "O-of course, Captain... H-Here, please have this! Think of it as an apology gift!"
"Good choice... veeery good choice." The pirate captain chuckles and starts counting your scrap.

destroyed.text: No matter how influential they were, at the end of the day they were still pirates. As you watch their ill-gotten station crumble to dust, you breath a sigh of relief that they won't make this sector even more of a hellish place.

event.text: As you scrap the station's debris you find what seems to be the pirate's safe. Inside it are crates containing all the resources you gave them and more!
As you scrap the station's debris you find what seems to be the pirate's safe. Inside it are crates containing a bunch of extra resources!

deadCrew.text: Not even the toughest pirates can stop you now. That's one less plague to torment this sector.

event.text: As you scrap the station's rooms you find what seems to be the pirate's safe. Inside of it are crates containing all the resources you gave them and more!
As you scrap the station's rooms you find what seems to be the pirate's safe. Inside it are crates containing a bunch of extra resources!

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_PIRATE_STATION
event.text: As soon as you land you are hailed by a nearby elite pirate sation. "Oi! Feddy boy! You think you can just land on *our* beacon and walk around like you own the place?! Give us 50 scrap and some missiles and drone parts or we might need to teach you a lesson on good manners!"
This outlaws shouldn't be allowed to stay in Federation territory with stations like those! Take those bastards down!

The pirate's eyes widen before they take a step back, hands raised defensevly. "O-Oh... y-yes! O-Of course! P-Please, take this! Call it a uh... apology gift! P-Please don't ruin my reputation... i-i don't want to go back to piloting Union shuttles..."

"Good. See? Wasn't this so much easier than fighting?" The pirate captain chuckles, but doesn't cut the feed. He grins and turns to his second in command to the right of him. "Say, Joie, what if we used some of that good old pirate freedom that we have been promised when we defacted?" You can't see the other pirate but you can hear them chuckling loudly.
"Sorry, little Feddy buddy, but it seems your scrap isn't enough. No, we want something a lot more valuable than that... how about your ship and crew?" The pirate smirks and powers on their weapons! You might be able to recover your wasted goods if you kill them.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_MISTAKE
event.text: With how frantic this sector is for those enlisted in the Federation's fleet, it's no wonder that sometimes mistakes are bound to happen. Case in point, the Unity ship that mistook you for a rebel spy.

choice.text: Attempt to clear up the missunderstanding.

event.text: You try to convince the Unity ship of your innocence, but your words fall on deaf ears. There's no avoiding this fight!
You somehow say the right words to avoid a fight. The Unity ship apologises and even sends some supplies to help your mission.

The sight of Admiral Tully immediate puts the Unity captain at ease. "Ah, Admiral Tully. It's good to see you on the front lines. I'm sorry for accusing you of being a Rebel; I will be more careful in the future. Please, have our spare resources. We also have a skilled Engi on board that might be able to give your ship some repairs as well."
If they want a fight so bad, then it's about time you show them what happens to traitors!

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_FEDERATION_AUTO
event.text: While generally relaible, it seems someone must have messed with this auto's sensors, as it seems to think you are a threat!
Without any skilled engineer aboard your ship, it's no surprise the repair failed. A fight is inevitable!
By some miracle you manage to undo whatever error there was with its sensors. The ship now sees you as an ally and gives you some goodies.
Your Engi is used to working with Federation auto ships, so they manage to fix the auto's faulty sensors in no time! The auto even gives you some extra goodies now that it knows you are an ally!

Your Engineer tries their best, but they struggle to work with the different Federation standards for the auto. While attempting to reset the auto's threat list, they acidentally connect the wrong wire to the wrong place, causing a chain reaction that quickly destroys the auto. At least you can scrap its remains...
You can't risk a Federation auto with its sensors broken continuing to be loose in such a dangerous sector, it will do more harm than good.

destroyed.text: Even the Hive's strongest ships have their limits, and it seems the enemy Bishop has finally reached theirs. With one last crack the ship explodes into pieces.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Bishop ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Mantis Bishops are some of the best warriors the multiverse has to offer. They capture the perfect mix of speed and power. No matter how mighty, though, all warriors must fall one day.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Bishop ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_COALITION_DOUBLE
event.text: You catch the conversation between two ships, a Bishop and a Knight. The topic at first seems to consist of their favorite holidays, with the Slug prefering the Crown Theft Festival and the Mantis, Renegade Day. However, as soon as they notice you, they quickly shift topics. "Ah! Slanky! Seem we have eaves dropper!" The Bishop speaks in a threatening tone while the Slug just chuckles. "And you know what we do to eavessssdroppers right? You got thissss, right Trakitep?" the Slug chuckles again, with the Mantis nodding and powering on their weapons.

destroyed.text: No matter how imposing Night's ships are, they lack the discipline and training that other elites tend to get. Their dirty tactics clearly failed them today as their ship breaks apart at the seams.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Knight ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

deadCrew.text: Knight crew are extremely resilient and are equipped with the best tech the Kleptocracy has to offer. Still, they die like any others.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Knight ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_COALITION_DOUBLE_MANTIS_WIN
event.text: The Knight looks devastated. They blink for a few moments before gritting their teeth and powering on their weapons. "You.... you... YOU PIECE OF CRAP RENEGADE! I WILL TEAR YOU APART FOR WHAT YOU DID TO MY FRIEND!"

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_COALITION_DOUBLE_SLUG_WIN
event.text: With the Knight out of the way, you are free to do a full search of the battlefield. You find what seems to be the map for the sector as well the flight plans for the Coalition in this sector. Maybe you can delay the rebels by forcing them to follow you through heavily reinforced Coalition routes?

destroyed.text: The automated Technician ship has been destroyed. Hopefully not too many lives were lost keeping it at bay before you arrived.

event.text: It seems the cargo of the Technician ship survived the fight. Inside is a functional weapon, which you bring back on board.

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_REBEL_TECHNICIAN_AUTO
event.text: As soon as you land you are immediately confronted by a Technician ship, weapons hot. However, when it hails you, you don't see anyone on the other side. Instead, a robotic voice speaks in a monotone and un-emotional manner. "FEDERATION. THREAT. ELIMINATE."

Suddenly, you are hailed by a small Technician shuttle, with a pair of twins on the other side. "Renegade! I never thought we would be happy to see one of you around! We were trying to install kind of base automation system on our ship but we acidentally made it go haywire! It killed most of our crew because it sees everyone as a threat! It destroyed our support auto and almost obliterated our escort MFK Ace ship! Please, don't let it kill anyone else!"

event.name: FR_HOSTILE_WARZONE_REBEL_TECHNICIAN_AUTO_WIN
event.text: The shuttle hails you "Man... it will suck having to explain what happened to our boss... But I suppose you did save us from a lot more trouble if that thing had gone unchallenged. Thank you! We don't have much on us, but we can give the fleet some, let's say, 'outdated' information on your whereabouts as a thank you. How does that sound?"

destroyed.text: The strange ship crumples before your very eyes, its hull tearing apart before something deep within the ship explodes, completely destroying everything around the ship, including any loot. At least your scrap returns are plentiful.

deadCrew.text:
Before the last soldier falls, they send out one last encrypted command to their ship's systems. Your crew quickly evacuates, thinking it was a self destruct command, but nothing happens.
Once you feel safe enough to return and explore the ship, you realize what the command did: it wiped out every single storage system in the ship completely clean. Seems like this mystery will live on for now.

event.text: At least it seems that their cargo survived the fight. You find the most expensive looking weapon and bring it back onboard.

event.name: FR_FINISH_BEACON_WARZONE_TOTALITY
event.text: As you finish your jump, you are met with a strange ship waiting for you. Their colors don't ring any bells and your database doesn't even return a name for whoever they represent. What you can tell for sure though is that they don't seem friendly at all: their weapons are already powering up!

Before a fight actually starts, your ship detects a signal being emitted from the ship. However, it's encrypted so heavily that it would take months for your ship to even have a chance to decypher what they just sent.

Your commander is hesitant at first, but soon agrees to help you. However, they refuse to decrypt the message to you, only telling you what the message was about. It seems this is an Assault vessel built specifically to track rogue Renegades like you. After a lot of persuasion, they agree to ask for the other vessel back down from the fight.
"Bird of Prey, this is Nested Owl. The Eagle is hunting its own. I repeat, the Eagle is hunting its own. Over."

After what feels like hours, the enemy ship's weapons power down. Another message is sent, but like before, your commander refuses to share it with you. All they say is that this specific ship will back down from hunting you.
You have no idea what this ship wants, but if it's a fight they are after, it's one they will get.
